ddls:
CREATE TABLE "financial_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each financial metric */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the financial metric (e.g., 1M, 2M, 3M) */,
  "metric_description" TEXT /* Description of the financial metric */,
  "metric_category" TEXT /* Category of the financial metric (e.g., interest rates, exchange rates, stock prices) */,
  "unit_of_measure" TEXT /* Unit of measure for the financial metric (e.g., percentage, dollars) */,
  "data_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of data collection (e.g., daily, weekly, monthly) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "time_series_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data point */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the data point */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* ID of the financial metric */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the financial metric on the given date */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data source */,
  "confidence_level" REAL /* Confidence level of the data point */,
  "is_estimated" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the value is estimated or actual */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update for this data point */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_time_series_data_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_time_series_data_metric_id FOREIGN KEY ("metric_id") REFERENCES financial_metrics ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., analyst, manager, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Access level of the user (e.g., read-only, read-write) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data point being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data point */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data point was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent (e.g., browser, device) used to access the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES time_series_data ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source */,
  "source_description" TEXT /* Description of the data source */,
  "api_endpoint" TEXT /* API endpoint for fetching data */,
  "last_synced" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last synchronization with the data source */,
  "frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of data synchronization (e.g., hourly, daily) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the data source (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_source_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data source */,
  "log_date" TEXT /* Date and time of the log entry */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the data fetch (e.g., success, failure) */,
  "message" TEXT /* Message or error details */,
  "duration" TEXT /* Duration of the data fetch and synchronization */,
  "data_volume" TEXT /* Volume of data fetched */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_source_logs_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_validations" (
  "validation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each validation rule */,
  "rule_name" TEXT /* Name of the validation rule */,
  "rule_description" TEXT /* Description of the validation rule */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* ID of the financial metric to which the rule applies */,
  "last_run" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last validation run */,
  "result" TEXT /* Result of the last validation run (e.g., pass, fail) */,
  "frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of validation rule execution */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity of the validation rule (e.g., critical, warning) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("validation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_validations_metric_id FOREIGN KEY ("metric_id") REFERENCES financial_metrics ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_errors" (
  "error_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each error */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data point with the error */,
  "error_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the error was detected */,
  "error_type" TEXT /* Type of error (e.g., data mismatch, missing value) */,
  "error_description" TEXT /* Description of the error */,
  "resolved_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the error was resolved */,
  "resolved_by" TEXT /* User who resolved the error */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data source */,
  "impact" TEXT /* Impact of the error (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("error_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_errors_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_errors_resolved_by FOREIGN KEY ("resolved_by") REFERENCES users ("user_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_errors_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES time_series_data ("data_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "report_name" TEXT /* Name of the report */,
  "report_description" TEXT /* Description of the report */,
  "created_by" TEXT /* User who created the report */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date and time the report was created */,
  "last_modified" TEXT /* Date and time the report was last modified */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the report (e.g., draft, published, archived) */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of report (e.g., daily, weekly, monthly) */,
  "last_run" TEXT /* Last run date and time of the report */,
  "next_run" TEXT /* Next run date and time of the report */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_name")
)
